description: A photograph capturing a rugged, dark grey rock cliff face under bright, clear blue skies. The dominant feature is the cliff's geological structure, characterized by prominent, steeply-angled strata appearing as numerous parallel bands. These layers are primarily vertical or sub-vertical and vary in shades of dark grey, with some sections showing brownish or reddish hues due to weathering or mineral content. The base of the cliff is composed of accumulated dark, angular rock debris (talus), which transitions into a sandy foreground containing scattered larger dark rocks. A bright sun glare is visible in the upper left quadrant of the sky, indicating direct sunlight and highlighting the textures and layering of the rock formation.
